info.name: Discovery Hub API
info.description: Complete API reference for the Discovery Hub platform.

request.description: Register a new church member. All fields except firstname, lastname, email and password are optional. Set joinWorkforce=true to flag interest in joining the workforce (admin must still formally promote the member).
Authenticate any user (MEMBER, WORKER, ADMIN). The test script auto-saves access_token and refresh_token to collection variables.
Exchange a refresh token for a new access token. Pass the refresh token in the Authorization header.
Invalidate current session. Returns 204 No Content.
Returns the authenticated user's profile including workerProfile and department if applicable.
Request a 6-digit OTP reset code. Rate-limited (default 3 attempts/hr). Always returns the same message regardless of whether the email exists, to avoid account enumeration.
Verify OTP and set a new password. Marks the OTP as used, invalidates the current session, and sends a confirmation email. The user must log in fresh after this.
ADMIN only. Returns the admin's own profile.

item.name: Members
item.description: All endpoints require ADMIN role unless noted.

query.description: Filter by role: MEMBER | WORKER | ADMIN (optional)
Filter by worker status: ACTIVE | INACTIVE (optional)

request.description: All fields are optional. Only provided fields are updated.
Creates a WorkerProfile for the member, assigns them to a department and sets role to WORKER. departmentId is required; profession and yearJoinedWorkforce are optional.
Removes the WorkerProfile and resets the member's role back to MEMBER. Returns 204 No Content.

Update a worker's profile fields. All fields are optional. Pass `"secondaryDepartmentId": null` to remove the secondary department assignment. Omitting `secondaryDepartmentId` entirely leaves the current value unchanged. ADMIN only.
Activate or deactivate a member. status: ACTIVE | INACTIVE. Returns 204.
Generates a new random password and emails it to the member. The member will need to change it on next login.

Any authenticated user (MEMBER or WORKER) can check in. location is optional for members. Workers providing location will have distance validated if ENFORCE_DISTANCE_CHECK=true. Members are always marked PRESENT; workers are PRESENT or LATE depending on workerLateOffsetSeconds.

response.name: 400 Already checked in
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "You have already checked in for this service." }

request.description: Returns the authenticated user's own attendance records. Available to all roles.

query.description: Required. UUID of the service slot.
Optional for WORKER (auto-resolved from profile). Required for ADMIN.

request.description: WORKER only (department lead). Returns attendance for their department for a given slot.
Returns PRESENT/LATE/ABSENT/ON_LEAVE counts for a single service slot.
Returns top workers ranked by present+late count within the time window.

query.description: Optional for WORKER (auto-resolved from profile). Required for ADMIN.

request.description: Returns attendance records for the caller's department for a specific event. Includes all service slots within that event. WORKER only (resolves the caller's department from their profile).
ADMIN only. Each slot references a configId (which carries a defaultVenue). Supply an optional venueOverrideId on individual slots to override the config venue for just that slot.
Minimal single-event example — one slot with a configId. At least one serviceSlot is required; slots without a configId will fail at check-in time.

Creates one event record per recurrence date up to recurrenceEndDate (max 1 year ahead). serviceSlots are required (at least one) and must include a configId. The same slot template is stamped on every occurrence — all share the same config. Delete future occurrences in bulk via DELETE /events/recurring/:recurringEventId.

Updates event metadata. When `serviceSlots` is provided (min 1 entry), ALL existing slots are replaced with the new ones — include `configId` on each slot to preserve config linkage. Omit `serviceSlots` entirely to leave existing slots unchanged. ADMIN only.
ADMIN only. Cannot delete past events.

variable.description: The shared recurringEventId UUID from the event series

request.description: ADMIN only. Deletes all future events in a recurring series (today and after).

body.raw:
{
  "name": "Default Sunday Config",
  "description": "Standard check-in config for Sunday services",
  "defaultVenueId": "{{venueId}}",
  "workerCheckinStartOffsetSeconds": -5400,
  "workerLateOffsetSeconds": 0,
  "memberCheckinStartOffsetSeconds": -1800,
  "checkinStopOffsetSeconds": 3600,
  "allowedDistanceInMeters": 150
}

request.description: Offsets are in seconds relative to slot startTime. Negative = before slot starts. Constraint: workerLateOffset > workerCheckinStartOffset and checkinStopOffset > workerLateOffset. defaultVenueId is required — create a Venue first and reference it here.
Fails if any service slot is still assigned to this config.
Returns the list of all valid DepartmentKeyEnum values. Use this to populate key-selection dropdowns when creating or updating a department. No auth required beyond a valid JWT.
Public (JWT only). Returns paginated list of departments.

ADMIN only. The optional `key` field links this department to a module-access category (DepartmentKeyEnum). Multiple departments may share the same key. Omit `key` (or pass null) if the department does not gate any module. Example keys: SUNDAY_SCHOOL, CHILDREN_CHURCH, MEDIA.
ADMIN only. All fields are optional. The `key` field can be updated to change module access. Set to null to remove module gating.
Assigns a worker as department head or assistant. type: 'head' | 'assistant'. The worker must have this department as their primary department to be assigned as HOD.
Paginated list of all workers whose primary department is this one. Each worker entry includes a `leadRole` field ("head" | "assistant" | null). ADMIN only.

query.description: Optional for WORKER (auto-resolved from profile). Required for ADMIN.

request.description: Returns aggregated stats for the department the caller leads (HOD or D-HOD): total/active/inactive worker counts, 30-day attendance %, and workers currently on approved or pending leave. WORKER (HOD/D-HOD) only — throws 403 if caller is not a department lead.
WORKER only. Dates must be in format: YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm. Creates a PENDING leave request.
ADMIN only. status: APPROVED | REJECTED.
WORKER only. Can only delete own PENDING leave requests.
WORKER only. Returns leave requests from members in the same department.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "New Believers Class",
  "type": "BELIEVERS",
  "description": "Foundation class for new members",
  "facilitatorId": "{{memberId}}",
  "startDate": "2026-07-01",
  "endDate": "2026-09-30"
}

request.description: type: BELIEVERS | BAPTISMAL | WORKERS_IN_TRAINING | BIBLE_COLLEGE

response.name: 400 Already enrolled
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "Member is already enrolled in this class" }

request.description: status: IN_PROGRESS | COMPLETED | CANCELLED
Returns all class enrollments for the authenticated user.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Church Picnic — July 20th",
  "body": "Join us for our annual church picnic at Tarkwa Bay Beach. Bring your family!",
  "audience": "ALL",
  "publishedAt": "2026-07-10T08:00:00.000Z",
  "expiresAt": "2026-07-21T00:00:00.000Z"
}

request.description: audience: ALL | WORKERS_ONLY | DEPARTMENT | INDIVIDUAL. If audience=DEPARTMENT, departmentId is required. If audience=INDIVIDUAL, targetMemberId (UUID) is required. publishedAt defaults to now if omitted.
Sends a private announcement visible only to the specified member. targetMemberId is required when audience=INDIVIDUAL.

query.description: Required for workers to receive department-targeted announcements (optional for members)

request.description: Returns visible, non-expired announcements for the current user's role. MEMBER sees ALL. WORKER sees ALL + WORKERS_ONLY + their department. Expired announcements are excluded.
type path param: child_naming | child_dedication | marriage
type field is required to identify the note type. Other fields are optional (partial update).

query.description: Start date YYYY-MM-DD (optional, defaults to 30 days ago)
End date YYYY-MM-DD (optional, defaults to today)

item.name: Venues
item.description: Named, reusable venue records. Create a venue once; reference its ID in event configs (defaultVenueId) or individual service slots (venueOverrideId). ADMIN: create, update, delete. Any authenticated user: list, get by ID.

request.description: Creates a new venue. The test script auto-saves the returned id to the venueId collection variable. name must be unique. latitude and longitude are required.
Any authenticated user. Returns all venues paginated, ordered by name.
All fields are optional. Only provided fields are updated.
Fails with 400 if the venue is referenced as a defaultVenue by any event config. Slot-level venueOverrides pointing to this venue are automatically cleared (SET NULL).

response.name: 400 In use
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "Cannot delete this venue — it is referenced by one or more event configs or slots." }

request.description: Any authenticated user. Returns profile, streak, rank, period stats, recent attendance, upcoming events, and class enrollments.

WORKER or ADMIN role. Includes streak, rank, period stats, and isDepartmentLead flag. If isDepartmentLead=true, also includes departmentLeadDetails with departmentAttendancePercentage and pending leave count.

ADMIN only. Returns total member/worker/admin counts, check-ins today, workerAttendancePercentage, congregationAttendancePercentage, departmentAttendanceSummary (per-department breakdown), newMemberRegistrationsTrend, weekly trend, top absent workers, members not seen recently, upcoming events, and pending leave count.

item.name: Sunday School
item.description: Session-based attendance for permanent Sunday School classes. Access requires ADMIN role, a worker whose primary or secondary department has key=SUNDAY_SCHOOL, or the appointed class teacher. Self-mark endpoints are open to any enrolled member when the window is open.

request.description: ADMIN or SS-dept worker. teacherId is optional — links an existing member as the class teacher.
ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or the class teacher. All fields are optional.
ADMIN only.
Any authenticated user.
Any authenticated user.
ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or class teacher. Permanently assigns a member to the class. Assignment is unique per (member, class).
ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or class teacher.
ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or class teacher.
ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or class teacher. sessionDate must be unique per class. selfMarkOpen defaults to false.
ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or class teacher. Flips selfMarkOpen between true and false. When open, enrolled members can self-mark their attendance.
Any authenticated user. Caller must be enrolled in the class and the session's selfMarkOpen must be true. Marks the caller PRESENT with markedByTeacher=false.

response.name: 400 Window closed
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "Self-mark window is not open for this session." }

response.name: 400 Not enrolled
response.body: { "statusCode": 400, "message": "You are not enrolled in this class." }

request.description: ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or class teacher. Upserts attendance for multiple members in a single call. status: PRESENT | ABSENT | EXCUSED. Sets markedByTeacher=true.
ADMIN, SS-dept worker, or class teacher. Returns the full attendance roster for the session including members with no record yet.

response.name: 200 OK

request.description: Paginated list of all sessions for a class, newest first. Requires SS department membership or class teacher role. WORKER/ADMIN.
Returns a single session with its class loaded. WORKER/ADMIN.
Deletes the session and all attendance records for it (CASCADE). Only the class teacher or SS staff can delete. WORKER/ADMIN.

item.name: Children Church
item.description: Security check-in/check-out system for children. Access requires ADMIN role or a worker whose primary or secondary department has key=CHILDREN_CHURCH. Age group and class group management is ADMIN-only.

request.description: ADMIN only. Age ranges are in months (inclusive). displayOrder controls sort order in listings.
ADMIN only. All fields are optional.
ADMIN only.
Any authenticated user. Returns age groups ordered by displayOrder.

ADMIN only. Iterates all registered children and reassigns each to the correct ageGroup and classGroup based on their current date of birth and the latest age-group rules. Run this after changing age-group boundaries.
ADMIN only. capacity and teacherNote are optional.
ADMIN only.
ADMIN only.

query.description: Optional. Filter class groups by age group UUID.

request.description: ADMIN or CC-dept worker.
ADMIN or CC-dept worker. ageGroup and classGroup are automatically assigned from dateOfBirth. photoUrl, specialNotes, and registeredByMemberId are optional.
ADMIN or CC-dept worker. All fields optional.
ADMIN or CC-dept worker. Returns full profile including ageGroup, classGroup, and guardians.
Paginated check-in/check-out history for a specific child, newest first. Includes the worker who performed each check-in (checkedInBy). WORKER/ADMIN.
Lists children, optionally filtered by name and/or classGroupId. Omit both filters to return all children. WORKER/ADMIN.

ADMIN or CC-dept worker. relationship: MOTHER | FATHER | GRANDPARENT | SIBLING | UNCLE | AUNT | FAMILY_FRIEND | OTHER. email and memberId are optional. Pickup notification emails are sent to guardian.email ?? guardian.member.email.
ADMIN or CC-dept worker.
ADMIN or CC-dept worker.
ADMIN or CC-dept worker. Generates a unique 6-char pickupCode and emails it to all registered guardians. serviceSlotId, droppedOffByGuardianId, and droppedOffByName are optional.

variable.description: The 6-char pickup code printed on the child's tag

request.description: ADMIN or CC-dept worker. Returns the matching ChildCheckIn record including child and guardian details for the pickup verification step.

response.name: 404 Not Found
response.body: { "statusCode": 404, "message": "No active check-in found for code ABC123" }

request.description: ADMIN or CC-dept worker. Validates the pickup code, sets status to CHECKED_OUT, and records the pickup time. pickedUpByGuardianId is optional if a name is sufficient.
ADMIN or CC-dept worker. Sets status to FLAGGED and records the reason. Flagged check-ins require manual resolution.

query.description: Optional. Filter by class group UUID.

request.description: ADMIN or CC-dept worker. Returns all check-ins with status CHECKED_IN or FLAGGED, optionally filtered by classGroupId.
ADMIN only. Returns all check-in records (any status) for a given service slot.
